data.title: Gamesquare projects 20%-25% organic growth in 2025 amid strategic divestitures and SaaS advancements
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: GameSquare Holdings, Inc. (GAME) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Justin Kenna highlighted the completion of the FaZe Clan integration and the successful divestiture of FaZe Media for over $39 million, which eliminated $10 million in debt and restructured operations to focus on profitability. The divestiture also positioned FaZe Clan Esports as a standalone entity, which achieved $7.3 million in revenue and was EBITDA positive for the second half of 2024.

  • The company announced a strategic focus on achieving positive adjusted EBITDA and cash flow in 2025, supported by organic growth initiatives targeting a 20%-25% increase in sales. Growth is anticipated from managed services within the SaaS and advertising business, along with the agency and media divisions.

  • CFO Mike Munoz reported pro forma revenue of $102 million in 2024, a 7.6% year-over-year increase. Improved gross margins of 15.3%, up from 14.7% in the prior year, were attributed to a more profitable revenue mix and cost reductions.

  • GameSquare's SaaS business, led by Stream Hatchet and Sideqik, demonstrated significant advancements, including the launch of an AI-powered influencer discovery tool and signing its largest contract with Capcom for the launch of Monster Hunter Wilds.

Outlook

  • CEO Kenna projected 2025 annual pro forma revenue between $100 million and $105 million, representing a year-over-year growth of at least 33% excluding FaZe Media’s 2024 contribution.

  • Gross margins are expected to increase to 20%-25%, supported by a more profitable revenue mix and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses are forecast to decline by $15 million in 2025 due to the divestiture of FaZe Media and other cost-saving measures.

  • Key growth drivers include expanded managed services offerings in the SaaS business, stronger pipelines in agency and media, and the acceleration of FaZe Clan Esports monetization efforts.

Financial Results

  • Pro forma adjusted EBITDA loss for 2024 improved to $19.8 million, a $26.3 million reduction compared to 2023. This improvement reflects significant cost reductions and efficiency gains.

  • The agency and media business demonstrated strong performance, with Zoned achieving a 115% year-over-year revenue increase driven by in-game world-building projects.

  • The company’s experiences business delivered a 150% revenue increase from Q3 to Q4 2024 and aims to multiply annual revenue fivefold in 2025.

Q&A

  • Greg Gibas, Northland Securities: Questioned growth expectations for 2025. CEO Kenna explained that growth is driven by robust pipelines across all segments, including SaaS and managed services, agency and media partnerships, and new licensing opportunities within FaZe Esports. He emphasized that several deals are at advanced stages, providing confidence in meeting revenue targets.

  • Jack Vander Aarde, Maxim Group: Inquired about the 2025 revenue guidance details. CEO Kenna clarified that the guidance excludes FaZe Media’s contributions and is supported by locked-in contracts and recurring revenue, with significant growth expected from FaZe Esports and the SaaS business.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism about the company's growth potential and operational progress, pressing for clarity on cost reductions and revenue drivers. Their tone was slightly positive, reflecting confidence in the outlined strategy.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly in discussing the divestiture of FaZe Media and the 2025 growth outlook. Phrases such as "we are confident" and "we believe" were frequently used to emphasize their expectations for profitability.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management demonstrated increased confidence, supported by the successful execution of strategic initiatives and the improved financial outlook.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 reflects a shift from a broader focus in Q3 2024 to a more targeted strategy emphasizing profitability and organic growth in Q4.

  • The Q4 call detailed the completed divestiture of FaZe Media, a major development since Q3, which improved the company’s financial position.

  • Analysts in Q4 were more focused on revenue growth potential and profitability timelines compared to Q3, where cost reductions and integration efforts dominated discussions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified challenges related to market dynamics and the execution of multiple large-scale contracts as potential risks.

  • The highly competitive and evolving nature of the gaming industry requires continuous innovation and strategic alignment to maintain growth momentum.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the potential impact of macroeconomic conditions on advertising budgets and project timelines.

Final Takeaway

GameSquare Holdings, Inc. demonstrated significant progress in Q4 2024, highlighted by the divestiture of FaZe Media and a strategic focus on profitability for 2025. The company is targeting 20%-25% organic revenue growth while improving gross margins and reducing operating costs. Management's confidence is bolstered by strong pipelines across its SaaS, agency, and esports divisions, positioning GameSquare for a pivotal year of financial and operational achievements.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nauticus Robotics outlines $16M+ revenue target for 2025 with expanded offshore operations
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Nauticus Robotics, Inc. (KITT) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO John Gibson highlighted the company’s transformation from a research and development entity to a commercially focused organization. He stated that 2024 marked the first commercial offshore operations of the Aquanaut vehicle, with legacy contracts proving the technology's viability and a pipeline of new contracts being developed for 2025.
  • Gibson announced the acquisition of SeaTrepid, describing it as a "game changer" that broadens customer access and reduces customer concentration. He also emphasized the integration of ToolKITT software, which could enable autonomous operations for ROVs and reduce subsea operational costs.
  • The CEO noted the upcoming release of an Olympic Arm variant, which will make Aquanaut the only autonomous underwater vehicle with autonomous manipulators.
  • Interim CFO Victoria Hay disclosed the completion of a restatement for 2024 financials, attributing the adjustments to a correction in the accounting treatment of a January 2024 debt transaction. She stated that the company exchanged $110.3 million of debt into Series A preferred stock, which significantly improved the balance sheet.
  • Hay reported a 2024 revenue figure of $1.8 million, which was down from 2023 due to reduced government contracts, but highlighted the first-time recognition of commercial revenue from the Aquanaut vehicle.

Outlook

  • Gibson projected significant revenue growth for 2025, targeting over $16 million, with potential for upside if additional assets are deployed. He noted a seven-month high season for offshore operations and highlighted high demand for the company’s technologies.
  • Management discussed leveraging the SeaTrepid acquisition to integrate ToolKITT software into ROVs and emphasized expanding capabilities for both nearshore and offshore operations.
  • The company aims to demonstrate the operational capabilities of Aquanaut and ToolKITT in deeper waters of up to 3,000 meters, broadening its market reach.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for 2024 was reported at $1.8 million, primarily driven by the first commercial deployments of the Aquanaut vehicle. This marked a shift from the company’s historical reliance on government contracts.
  • Operating expenses totaled $24.9 million in 2024, reflecting a decrease of $36.8 million compared to 2023 due to cost controls and reduced headcount.
  • The company reported a net loss of $134.9 million for 2024, with a significant portion attributed to noncash impacts from debt restructuring activities.
  • Cash at year-end stood at $1.2 million, supported by equity financing and at-the-market offerings that raised $19.4 million in Q1 2025.

Q&A

  • Kunal Madhukar, Water Tower Research: Questioned the revenue potential for 2025 and its drivers. CEO Gibson indicated confidence in achieving over $16 million in revenue, with additional upside if further assets are deployed.
  • Madhukar: Inquired about differentiation in the competitive landscape. Gibson underlined key features such as manipulators for autonomous vehicles, untethered operations, and advanced payload capabilities, which set the company apart from competitors like Oceaneering.
  • Madhukar: Asked about capital requirements for expanding operations. Gibson mentioned plans for nondilutive contract-based lending to finance additional assets.
  • Unidentified Analyst: Queried the dynamics driving the active offshore season. Management attributed this to stable oil prices, regulatory drivers, and revitalized infrastructure investments.
  • Unidentified Analyst: Asked if the company’s strategy had shifted. Gibson emphasized continued focus on high-demand, high-margin opportunities without pivoting from its core strategy.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on revenue potential and differentiation in the competitive landscape. Questions reflected interest in financial sustainability and the scalability of operations.
  • Management displayed confidence during prepared remarks and Q&A. Gibson frequently emphasized the company’s unique capabilities and market positioning, while Hay detailed improvements in financial positioning.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management appeared more optimistic about near-term revenue growth and operational readiness.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance language shifted from cautious optimism in Q3 2024 to more definitive projections in Q4 2024, with a clear target of $16 million for 2025.
  • Strategic focus transitioned from proof-of-concept operations to scaling commercial deployments and leveraging acquisitions like SeaTrepid.
  • Analysts in the current quarter displayed a stronger focus on revenue potential and differentiation compared to prior queries about operational milestones.
  • Management maintained a consistent tone of confidence, but the Q4 call highlighted an increased emphasis on customer engagement and pipeline development.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the challenges of securing sufficient capital to scale operations and develop additional assets. Plans for contract-based lending were discussed as a mitigation strategy.
  • Analysts raised concerns about competitive pressures and the company’s ability to maintain differentiation in a growing market for underwater autonomous vehicles.
  • Gibson noted the importance of continuing to meet NASDAQ compliance and emphasized the company’s efforts to improve financial stability.

Final Takeaway

Nauticus Robotics demonstrated significant progress in transitioning to a commercial entity, with 2024 marking the first revenue-generating operations for the Aquanaut vehicle. The company’s $16 million revenue target for 2025 reflects confidence in scaling operations, supported by customer interest and recent acquisitions. Management’s emphasis on differentiation and high-margin opportunities positions the company for growth, but challenges such as capital requirements and competitive pressures remain focal points for stakeholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Alcoa expects $90M hit from tariffs in Q2; CEO says impractical to boost capacity in U.S.
data.description:
Alcoa (NYSE:AA) -1.8% post-market Wednesday after reporting better than expected Q1 adjusted earnings but saying it expects costs related to U.S. tariffs on aluminum imports from Canada will rise to $90M in the current quarter, after incurring $20M in costs stemming from tariffs during Q1.

"Approximately 70% of our aluminum produced in Canada is destined for U.S. customers and is now subject to 25% tariff costs... Currently the net annual result is approximately $100 million negative for our business," CEO William Oplinger said during the company's earnings conference call.

Alcoa (NYSE:AA) sources some of its input materials from Chinese suppliers, and Oplinger said he expects the high U.S. tariffs on China will boost the company's annual costs by $10M-$15M, "as there are no suitable replacement suppliers."

Higher prices for domestically produced aluminum will partially offset the costs, Oplinger said, but it would be impractical to increase production in the U.S. in the short term, which has been one of the Trump administration's top goals in raising tariffs.

The U.S. currently imports more than 4M metric tons/year of aluminum, mostly from Canada, but the U.S. would need to build at least five new smelters to close its trade deficit for the metal, which likely would take up to a decade, cost billions of dollars and require additional energy production equivalent to that of seven nuclear reactors, the CEO said.

"Until additional smelting capacity is built in the U.S., the most efficient aluminum supply chain is Canadian aluminum flowing into the U.S.," Oplinger said on the call, adding that Alcoa (AA) would not build new capacity solely because of tariffs.

Alcoa (AA) executives have said previously that tariffs could result in the loss of 100K jobs in the U.S. aluminum industry.

data.title: Oil prices rise as U.S. sanctions Chinese importers of Iranian crude
data.description:
Crude oil futures defied a tech-led slide in equities and posted solid gains Wednesday, as the Trump administration issued new sanctions targeting Iran's oil exports, including against a China-based "teapot refinery," the latest U.S. policy move that has threatened to drive Iran's oil exports down to zero.

The move comes as the U.S. re-launched negotiations with Iran over its nuclear program this month, while Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said the country's right to enrich uranium is not negotiable, ahead of the next round of talks in Rome.

Price also were supported by dollar weakness and a Bloomberg report that China might negotiate with the U.S. on tariffs under certain conditions.

But macroeconomic headwinds likely will limit any oil price recovery despite some optimism around potential tariff rollbacks, while rising U.S. crude inventories suggest ample supply, George Pavel of Naga.com Middle East wrote, as "without stronger support from broader market conditions, crude oil prices could remain near their lows."

Meanwhile, the latest data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration showed commercial crude oil inventories rose by 515K barrels to 442.9M barrels in the week ended April 11, but both gasoline and distillate fuel inventories fell by 2M barrels and 1.9M barrels, respectively.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for May delivery closed +1.8% to $62.47/bbl, and front-month June Brent crude (CO1:COM) also ended +1.8% to $65.85/bbl, the highest settlement value for both benchmarks since April 3; front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for May delivery finished -2.4% to $3.247/MMBtu, its seventh loss in the past nine sessions.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Energy stocks (NYSEARCA:XLE) easily topped the day's sector leaderboard on the S&P 500, comprising four of the day's top six gainers on the index: APA Corp. (APA) +3.2%, Targa Resources (TRGP) +2.7%, Devon Energy (DVN) +2.7%, Diamondback Energy (FANG) +2.4%.

Citi analysts said they expect the fallout from trade tariffs will push Brent crude oil prices lower to $60/bbl in coming months, setting up a buying opportunity for a recovery as producers adjust supply.

Citi predicts a pickup in crude prices in this year's H2, expecting OPEC+ to pause the unwinding of voluntary production cuts, and seeing U.S. and Canadian output easing late this year "even as greater sanctions pressures on Iran and Venezuela could hit more oil barrels."

A supply response could help counterbalance weaker demand, "still offering some support to oil prices through the summer," Citi said, as it now sees oil demand growth this year of less than 700K bbl/day and keeps its 2026 demand growth estimate at 900K bbl/day.

data.title: WidePoint aims for EPS positivity in 2025 with $290M contract backlog
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: WidePoint Corporation (WYY) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Jin Kang, President and CEO, highlighted the company’s strong financial performance in 2024, achieving $142.6 million in revenues, a 35% increase compared to 2023. He noted that the company surpassed its adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow guidance, reporting $2.6 million and $2.5 million respectively.
  • Kang emphasized the significance of the company’s FedRAMP authorized status for its Intelligent Technology Management System (ITMS), achieved after more than three years of effort. This milestone positions WidePoint to access new federal contracts, including DHS CWMS 3.0, the Decennial Census 2030, and NASA SEWP VI.
  • The integration of IT Authority (ITA) into WidePoint was completed, streamlining operations and enhancing upselling and cross-selling opportunities with existing customers.
  • Jason Holloway, Chief Revenue Officer, discussed the launch of new technologies such as MobileAnchor and M365 Analyzer, which have already secured contracts with federal agencies. He also highlighted the company’s strong position in pursuing the DHS CWMS 3.0 recompete and mentioned the recent $2.5 million annual task order under the US Navy Spiral 4 contract.
  • CFO Robert George reported Q4 revenues of $37.7 million, a significant year-over-year increase. He detailed improvements in gross profit, adjusted EBITDA, and free cash flow metrics, showing a 229% increase in adjusted EBITDA and a 933% improvement in free cash flow for the year.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated its goal for achieving positive earnings per share (EPS) for 2025, driven by strong contract backlog and new opportunities in both federal and commercial segments.
  • The company plans to focus on commercializing new solutions, particularly MobileAnchor and M365 Analyzer, and leveraging strategic partnerships to grow its market presence.
  • WidePoint aims to expand its $290 million contract backlog further and sees significant potential in projects like the DHS CWMS 3.0 recompete and the Alliant 3 contract.

Financial Results

  • WidePoint’s Q4 revenue was $37.7 million, up from $28.3 million in the previous year’s quarter. Full-year revenue reached $142.6 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $631,000, resulting in a full-year total of $2.6 million, marking a robust increase from $791,000 in 2023.
  • Free cash flow for the year was $2.5 million, a substantial improvement from negative $300,000 in 2023.
  • Gross profit for Q4 was $4.8 million, with a gross margin of 13%. Gross profit percentage, excluding carrier services, improved to 36% in Q4 from 32% in the prior year’s quarter.

Q&A

  • Scott Buck, H.C. Wainwright, asked about updates on MobileAnchor pilots. Jin Kang responded that the pilot with a federal transportation agency was completed successfully and further rollouts are planned.
  • Buck also inquired about potential impacts from federal budget cuts. Kang emphasized that most supported agencies are insulated from budget reductions and projected increased activity, especially with DHS and CBP.
  • Barry Sine, Litchfield Hills Research, raised questions about leveraging opportunities with the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). Kang described the company’s historical success in delivering cost savings, positioning WidePoint as a natural partner for efficiency initiatives.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed positive interest in the company's technological advancements, particularly MobileAnchor and FedRAMP, reflecting optimism about growth potential.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout the call, emphasizing achievements and future goals. Kang’s remarks on the company’s alignment with federal efficiency initiatives and its strong contract backlog reinforced this sentiment.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained optimistic, with an increased focus on showcasing technological differentiation and strategic opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company reported higher revenues and improved free cash flow in Q4 compared to Q3, continuing its growth trajectory.
  • Management’s confidence in technological advancements, such as MobileAnchor and ITMS, and their contribution to new business opportunities was emphasized more this quarter.
  • Analysts maintained their focus on federal opportunities, with additional interest in commercial pipeline developments.

Risks and Concerns

  • The company acknowledged potential risks related to federal budget cuts but reiterated its confidence in the essential nature of its services.
  • WidePoint is monitoring developments such as the consolidation of federal contracting under GSA and changes in acquisition regulations, which could impact future contract competitions.

Final Takeaway

WidePoint demonstrated strong financial performance in Q4 2024 and is positioned for continued growth, supported by a $290 million contract backlog and advancements like FedRAMP authorization for its ITMS. Management aims to achieve positive EPS in 2025 while expanding its commercial footprint and leveraging strategic partnerships. The company’s focus on cost-saving solutions aligns with federal efficiency priorities, bolstering its competitive position in key government and commercial markets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: French aerospace supplier Lauak is said to explore strategic investment
data.description:
Lauak Groupe, a family-owned aerospace supplier based in France, is considering bringing on outside investors as it looks to expand internationally, potentially through a full or partial sale of the business, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.

Chief Executive Mikel Charritton told the news outlet that “all options are on the table,” suggesting possibilities that include a minority investment from a private equity firm or a majority acquisition by an industrial partner.

Based in the Basque town of Hasparren, Lauak has drawn interest from several potential buyers, including India’s Lohia Aerospace Systems, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the discussions. Lohia, which specializes in composite manufacturing, represents India’s growing footprint in the global aerospace supply chain.

Charritton declined to confirm any names but acknowledged that talks are underway

Founded in 1975 by Jean-Marc Charritton, Lauak produces components, subassemblies and fully integrated systems for major aerospace firms such as Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY), Safran (OTCPK:SAFRF) (OTCPK:SAFRY), Collins Aerospace (RTX) and Bombardier (OTCQX:BDRBF) (OTCQX:BDRAF) (OTCPK:BOMBF). Over the years, the company has expanded steadily through acquisitions and now operates 10 manufacturing sites across France, Portugal, Mexico, Canada and India, employing roughly 1,800 people. Its annual revenue stands at around €210 million ($239 million).

With global supply chains in flux due to geopolitical tensions and shifting trade policies, Western aerospace companies are increasingly turning to countries like India and Mexico as alternatives to China. This trend is fueling a surge of interest in mid-sized suppliers with established production footprints in those regions.

data.title: Gold surges by most in five years as U.S.-China economic tensions keep climbing
data.description:
Gold futures soared by the biggest daily percentage gain since April 2020, when panicked investors were hoarding the metal at the outset of the pandemic, as the dollar fell and tech stocks tumbled after President Trump ordered an investigation into possible tariffs on U.S. critical minerals imports, marking another escalation in his dispute with global trade partners and an attempt to pressure China.

The latest tariff turmoil followed the previous day's news that giant AI chipmaker Nvidia was told by the Trump administration to restrict exports of its H20 AI chips to China, smacking the company's profits by $5.5B.

The dollar, meanwhile, fell against its rivals to hold near three-year lows hit last week, making gold more attractive for other currency holders.

Gold's latest rally comes as the U.S. looks to further isolate China from global trade, SP Angel analysts said, noting Treasury Secretary Bessent has pitched the idea of extracting concessions from U.S. trading partners to isolate China, and reports of potentially removing Chinese stocks from U.S. exchanges; the dollar has resumed its downward trend and ETF demand is picking up, further strengthening gold's appeal, the firm added.

Trump has signaled new tariffs are on the way despite a recent partial reprieve, U.S.-China tensions show no signs of easing, and the dollar has been dragged lower by the president's trade policies, with concerns mounting around the currency's ability to act as a safe-haven, adding to gold's appeal, Tradu.com's Nikos Tzabouras wrote.

SPDR Gold Trust (NYSEARCA:GLD), the world's largest gold ETF, has surpassed a record $100B in assets under management.

Front-month Comex gold for April delivery (XAUUSD:CUR) closed +3.3% to a new record high settlement of $3,326.60/oz - up more than $100/oz - in its largest one-day percentage gain since April 9, 2020, while front-month April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +2.2% to $32.926/oz, its best level in two weeks and eighth straight daily gain.

Gold has now gained 26.5% YTD - nearly $700/oz - while silver has increased 13.7% YTD.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Silver's rally should continue in the short term, though demand likely will ease by the end of 2025, according to a report by the Silver Institute.

Some silver investors have been disappointed with the metal's performance compared with gold's record-breaking run-up, which reflects gold's more generally accepted monetary properties and position as a diversifier, but the  gap could lessen over the next few months given silver's tailwinds, and as investors look for value given its relative underperformance compared with gold, the report said.

But "while ongoing uncertainties elsewhere, along with silver's healthy supply-demand conditions, will offer support, we do see prices easing back in late 2025," the report said.

data.title: Ashford Hospitality sees Q1 RevPAR rising 3.2% from year ago
data.description:
Ashford Hospitality Trust (NYSE:AHT) expects to post occupancy of about 68% for the first quarter of the year, it said on Wednesday.

Also for Q1, the hospital REIT sees its average daily rate coming in at $196, resulting in revenue per available room (RevPAR) of ~$133. That reflects a ~3.2% increase in comparable RevPAR from a year ago.

"We continue to see the benefits of our GRO AHT initiative across both the top and bottom lines," said Ashford Trust President and CEO Stephen Zsigray.

"We also continue to make progress with extending and refinancing our loans," he added. "While the past few weeks have introduced some uncertainty to industry-wide forecasts, we believe our focus on maximizing revenues and minimizing expenses across our portfolio will help us continue to outperform."

Comparable RevPAR gained ~3.8% Y/Y in January, +4.3% Y/Y in February and +1.8% Y/Y in March.

Ashford (NYSE:AHT) is slated to publish its full Q1 results on May 6 after market close.

data.title: SKK receives Nasdaq notification letter regarding minimum bid price deficiency
data.description:
• SKK Holdings (NASDAQ:SKK) on Wednesday said it has received a notification letter from Nasdaq, notifying the company that based on the closing bid price of the company for the period from February 28, 2025 to April 11, 2025, it no longer meets the continued listing requirement, to maintain a minimum bid price of $1 per share.
  • Pursuant to listing rules, the company has a compliance period of one hundred eighty calendar days, or until October 13, 2025, to regain compliance.
  • SKK -1.32% after hours to $0.75.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Liberty Energy GAAP EPS of $0.12 beats by $0.08, revenue of $977.46M beats by $29M
data.description:
• Liberty Energy press release (NYSE:LBRT): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.12 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $977.46M (-11.1% Y/Y) beats by $29M.
